best translation is the translation that survives round trip translation.\n\nSo we should by default find such formulation for translation, that is more likely and in most part will survive round trip translation.\n\nWe need to make sure that all our logic related to formal translation via meta language (between programming languages, between natural languages) all have round trip translation quality test.\n\nTranslation to meta language from any other language must have zero data loss, meaning, roundtrip from meta language and back must work always.\n\nYet we also need to test roundtrips from Rust to meta language to JavaScript (roundtrip via meta language between JavaScript and Rust), from JavaScript to meta language to Rust (and round trip). The same for Russian -> meta language -> English, English -> meta language -> Russian. So Rust <-> JavaScript, Russian <-> English and so on. We should support tests for all roundtrips between each separate language and meta language, and between each 2 languages we support via meta language.\n\nWe should never support direct translation without meta language, as doing so will be computationally unsolvable.\n\nFor example if we have 100 languages, translators between them will be 100 * 100 = 10 000, and if we have 100 languages + meta language, we will need only 200 translators (to and from meta language from each 100 languages).\n\nMake sure our vision, requirements, roadmap and contributing guide all do not contradict this, and all include all relevant text about the case.\n\nWe need to collect data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), list of each and all requirements from the issue, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).\n\nPlease plan and execute everything in this single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.","closed_by":null,"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/526/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/526/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null,"pinned_comment":null}🔍 Check #1:                 3:00:12 PM

best translation is the translation that survives round trip translation.\n\nSo we should by default find such formulation for translation, that is more likely and in most part will survive round trip translation.\n\nWe need to make sure that all our logic related to formal translation via meta language (between programming languages, between natural languages) all have round trip translation quality test.\n\nTranslation to meta language from any other language must have zero data loss, meaning, roundtrip from meta language and back must work always.\n\nYet we also need to test roundtrips from Rust to meta language to JavaScript (roundtrip via meta language between JavaScript and Rust), from JavaScript to meta language to Rust (and round trip). The same for Russian -\u003e meta language -\u003e English, English -\u003e meta language -\u003e Russian. So Rust \u003c-\u003e JavaScript, Russian \u003c-\u003e English and so on. We should support tests for all roundtrips between each separate language and meta language, and between each 2 languages we support via meta language.\n\nWe should never support direct translation without meta language, as doing so will be computationally unsolvable.\n\nFor example if we have 100 languages, translators between them will be 100 * 100 = 10 000, and if we have 100 languages + meta language, we will need only 200 translators (to and from meta language from each 100 languages).\n\nMake sure our vision, requirements, roadmap and contributing guide all do not contradict this, and all include all relevant text about the case.\n\nWe need to collect data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), list of each and all requirements from the issue, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).\n\nPlease plan and execute everything in this single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.","title":"Translation quality test"}

This removes the N² anti-pattern issue #526 explicitly forbids: the translator now stays at `N` formalizers + `N` renderers, so pairs that never had a hardcoded arm (Python → JavaScript, Rust → Go) translate through one shared meaning for free.\n- Added issue #526 round-trip translation quality requirements, solution notes, online research, and raw PR/issue snapshots under `docs/case-studies/issue-526/`.\n- Expanded natural-language translation coverage so every supported language surface formalizes to the meta language and every directed en/ru/hi/zh pair round-trips through the shared meaning (already routed through `TranslationPipeline`, not direct pairs).\n- Added Rust → JavaScript → Rust code translation coverage plus a `code_translation_routes_through_the_code_meta_language_not_direct_pairs` test that proves never-hardcoded pairs translate through one shared `meaning:` link.\n- Updated `VISION.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `ROADMAP.md`, `REQUIREMENTS.md`, and `CONTRIBUTING.md` so future translation work is governed by the same round-trip, meta-language rule.\n\n## Root obsolete practice fixed\n`translate_program` previously matched on the concrete `(source, target)` language pair — a direct pair-specific translator. Issue #526 states direct translation without a meta language is computationally unsolvable (N languages → N² translators). The fix formalizes any source fragment into a `CodeMeaning` (source language irrelevant), then renders that meaning into the requested target; no `(source, target)` arm survives.\n\n## Reproduction\nBefore the fix, the reproducer failed because Rust → JavaScript returned a translation gap instead of an add-function renderer, and cross pairs like Python → JavaScript were never supported:\n\n```bash\ncargo test --test unit rust_javascript_code_translation_round_trips_through_code_meaning -- --nocapture\ncargo test --test unit code_translation_routes_through_the_code_meta_language_not_direct_pairs -- --nocapture\n```\n\n## Verification\nPost-merge verification on branch `issue-526-215ae32f521b`:\n\n```bash\ncargo test --test unit translation_round_trip -- --nocapture\ncargo test --test unit translation_via_links -- --nocapture\ncargo test --test unit issue_526_translation_quality_documents_are_traceable -- --nocapture\ncargo fmt --check\nrust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs\ncargo clippy --all-targets --all-features\ncargo test\n```\n\n## Visuals\nNot applicable; this change is tests, translation helper behavior, and best translation is the translation that survives round trip translation.\n\nSo we should by default find such formulation for translation, that is more likely and in most part will survive round trip translation.\n\nWe need to make sure that all our logic related to formal translation via meta language (between programming languages, between natural languages) all have round trip translation quality test.\n\nTranslation to meta language from any other language must have zero data loss, meaning, roundtrip from meta language and back must work always.\n\nYet we also need to test roundtrips from Rust to meta language to JavaScript (roundtrip via meta language between JavaScript and Rust), from JavaScript to meta language to Rust (and round trip). The same for Russian -> meta language -> English, English -> meta language -> Russian. So Rust <-> JavaScript, Russian <-> English and so on. We should support tests for all roundtrips between each separate language and meta language, and between each 2 languages we support via meta language.\n\nWe should never support direct translation without meta language, as doing so will be computationally unsolvable.\n\nFor example if we have 100 languages, translators between them will be 100 * 100 = 10 000, and if we have 100 languages + meta language, we will need only 200 translators (to and from meta language from each 100 languages).\n\nMake sure our vision, requirements, roadmap and contributing guide all do not contradict this, and all include all relevant text about the case.\n\nWe need to collect data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), list of each and all requirements from the issue, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).\n\nPlease plan and execute everything in this single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.","closed_by":null,"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/526/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/526/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null,"pinned_comment":null}🔍 Check #2:                 3:02:19 PM

best translation is the translation that survives round trip translation.\n\nSo we should by default find such formulation for translation, that is more likely and in most part will survive round trip translation.\n\nWe need to make sure that all our logic related to formal translation via meta language (between programming languages, between natural languages) all have round trip translation quality test.\n\nTranslation to meta language from any other language must have zero data loss, meaning, roundtrip from meta language and back must work always.\n\nYet we also need to test roundtrips from Rust to meta language to JavaScript (roundtrip via meta language between JavaScript and Rust), from JavaScript to meta language to Rust (and round trip). The same for Russian -\u003e meta language -\u003e English, English -\u003e meta language -\u003e Russian. So Rust \u003c-\u003e JavaScript, Russian \u003c-\u003e English and so on. We should support tests for all roundtrips between each separate language and meta language, and between each 2 languages we support via meta language.\n\nWe should never support direct translation without meta language, as doing so will be computationally unsolvable.\n\nFor example if we have 100 languages, translators between them will be 100 * 100 = 10 000, and if we have 100 languages + meta language, we will need only 200 translators (to and from meta language from each 100 languages).\n\nMake sure our vision, requirements, roadmap and contributing guide all do not contradict this, and all include all relevant text about the case.\n\nWe need to collect data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), list of each and all requirements from the issue, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).\n\nPlease plan and execute everything in this single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.","title":"Translation quality test"}

This removes the N² anti-pattern issue #526 explicitly forbids: the translator now stays at `N` formalizers + `N` renderers, so pairs that never had a hardcoded arm (Python → JavaScript, Rust → Go) translate through one shared meaning for free.\n- Added issue #526 round-trip translation quality requirements, solution notes, online research, and raw PR/issue snapshots under `docs/case-studies/issue-526/`.\n- Expanded natural-language translation coverage so every supported language surface formalizes to the meta language and every directed en/ru/hi/zh pair round-trips through the shared meaning (already routed through `TranslationPipeline`, not direct pairs).\n- Added Rust → JavaScript → Rust code translation coverage plus a `code_translation_routes_through_the_code_meta_language_not_direct_pairs` test that proves never-hardcoded pairs translate through one shared `meaning:` link.\n- Updated `VISION.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `ROADMAP.md`, `REQUIREMENTS.md`, and `CONTRIBUTING.md` so future translation work is governed by the same round-trip, meta-language rule.\n\n## Root obsolete practice fixed\n`translate_program` previously matched on the concrete `(source, target)` language pair — a direct pair-specific translator. Issue #526 states direct translation without a meta language is computationally unsolvable (N languages → N² translators). The fix formalizes any source fragment into a `CodeMeaning` (source language irrelevant), then renders that meaning into the requested target; no `(source, target)` arm survives.\n\n## Reproduction\nBefore the fix, the reproducer failed because Rust → JavaScript returned a translation gap instead of an add-function renderer, and cross pairs like Python → JavaScript were never supported:\n\n```bash\ncargo test --test unit rust_javascript_code_translation_round_trips_through_code_meaning -- --nocapture\ncargo test --test unit code_translation_routes_through_the_code_meta_language_not_direct_pairs -- --nocapture\n```\n\n## Verification\nPost-merge verification on branch `issue-526-215ae32f521b`:\n\n```bash\ncargo test --test unit translation_round_trip -- --nocapture\ncargo test --test unit translation_via_links -- --nocapture\ncargo test --test unit issue_526_translation_quality_documents_are_traceable -- --nocapture\ncargo fmt --check\nrust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs\ncargo clippy --all-targets --all-features\ncargo test\n```\n\n## Visuals\nNot applicable; this change is tests, translation best translation is the translation that survives round trip translation.\n\nSo we should by default find such formulation for translation, that is more likely and in most part will survive round trip translation.\n\nWe need to make sure that all our logic related to formal translation via meta language (between programming languages, between natural languages) all have round trip translation quality test.\n\nTranslation to meta language from any other language must have zero data loss, meaning, roundtrip from meta language and back must work always.\n\nYet we also need to test roundtrips from Rust to meta language to JavaScript (roundtrip via meta language between JavaScript and Rust), from JavaScript to meta language to Rust (and round trip). The same for Russian -> meta language -> English, English -> meta language -> Russian. So Rust <-> JavaScript, Russian <-> English and so on. We should support tests for all roundtrips between each separate language and meta language, and between each 2 languages we support via meta language.\n\nWe should never support direct translation without meta language, as doing so will be computationally unsolvable.\n\nFor example if we have 100 languages, translators between them will be 100 * 100 = 10 000, and if we have 100 languages + meta language, we will need only 200 translators (to and from meta language from each 100 languages).\n\nMake sure our vision, requirements, roadmap and contributing guide all do not contradict this, and all include all relevant text about the case.\n\nWe need to collect data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), list of each and all requirements from the issue, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).\n\nPlease plan and execute everything in this single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.","closed_by":null,"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/526/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/526/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null,"pinned_comment":null}🔍 Check #3:                 3:04:27 PM

best translation is the translation that survives round trip translation.\n\nSo we should by default find such formulation for translation, that is more likely and in most part will survive round trip translation.\n\nWe need to make sure that all our logic related to formal translation via meta language (between programming languages, between natural languages) all have round trip translation quality test.\n\nTranslation to meta language from any other language must have zero data loss, meaning, roundtrip from meta language and back must work always.\n\nYet we also need to test roundtrips from Rust to meta language to JavaScript (roundtrip via meta language between JavaScript and Rust), from JavaScript to meta language to Rust (and round trip). The same for Russian -\u003e meta language -\u003e English, English -\u003e meta language -\u003e Russian. So Rust \u003c-\u003e JavaScript, Russian \u003c-\u003e English and so on. We should support tests for all roundtrips between each separate language and meta language, and between each 2 languages we support via meta language.\n\nWe should never support direct translation without meta language, as doing so will be computationally unsolvable.\n\nFor example if we have 100 languages, translators between them will be 100 * 100 = 10 000, and if we have 100 languages + meta language, we will need only 200 translators (to and from meta language from each 100 languages).\n\nMake sure our vision, requirements, roadmap and contributing guide all do not contradict this, and all include all relevant text about the case.\n\nWe need to collect data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), list of each and all requirements from the issue, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).\n\nPlease plan and execute everything in this single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.","title":"Translation quality test"}

{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/link-assistant/formal-ai/pulls/635","id":3990512685,"node_id":"PR_kwDOSbOoU87t2mQt","html_url":"https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/635","diff_url":"https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/635.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/635.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/635","number":635,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Add translation round-trip quality coverage","user":{"login":"konard","id":1431904,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0MzE5MDQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1431904?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/konard","html_url":"https://github.com/konard","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/konard/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/konard/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/konard/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/konard/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/konard/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/konard/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/konard/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/konard/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/konard/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Fixes #526\n\n## Summary\n- Reworked code translation (`translate_program`) to route through a language-neutral **code meta language** (`CodeMeaning` / `formalize_code_meaning` / `render_code_meaning`) instead of direct `(source, target)` match arms. This removes the N² anti-pattern issue #526 explicitly forbids: the translator now stays at `N` formalizers + `N` renderers, so pairs that never had a hardcoded arm (Python → JavaScript, Rust → Go) translate through one shared meaning for free.\n- Added issue #526 round-trip translation quality requirements, solution notes, online research, and raw PR/issue snapshots under `docs/case-studies/issue-526/`.\n- Expanded natural-language translation coverage so every supported language surface formalizes to the meta language and every directed en/ru/hi/zh pair round-trips through the shared meaning (already routed through `TranslationPipeline`, not direct pairs).\n- Added Rust → JavaScript → Rust code translation coverage plus a `code_translation_routes_through_the_code_meta_language_not_direct_pairs` test that proves never-hardcoded pairs translate through one shared `meaning:` link.\n- Updated `VISION.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `ROADMAP.md`, `REQUIREMENTS.md`, and `CONTRIBUTING.md` so future translation work is governed by the same round-trip, meta-language rule.\n\n## Root obsolete practice fixed\n`translate_program` previously matched on the concrete `(source, target)` language pair — a direct pair-specific translator. Issue #526 states direct translation without a meta language is computationally unsolvable (N languages → N² translators). The fix formalizes any source fragment into a `CodeMeaning` (source language irrelevant), then renders that meaning into the requested target; no `(source, target)` arm survives.\n\n## Reproduction\nBefore the fix, the reproducer failed because Rust → JavaScript returned a translation gap instead of an add-function renderer, and cross pairs like Python → JavaScript were never supported:\n\n```bash\ncargo test --test unit rust_javascript_code_translation_round_trips_through_code_meaning -- --nocapture\ncargo test --test unit code_translation_routes_through_the_code_meta_language_not_direct_pairs -- --nocapture\n```\n\n## Verification\nPost-merge verification on branch `issue-526-215ae32f521b`:\n\n```bash\ncargo test --test unit translation_round_trip -- --nocapture\ncargo test --test unit translation_via_links -- --nocapture\ncargo test --test unit issue_526_translation_quality_documents_are_traceable -- --nocapture\ncargo fmt --check\nrust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs\ncargo clippy --all-targets --all-features\ncargo test\n```\n\n## Visuals\nNot applicable; this change is tests, translation best translation is the translation that survives round trip translation.\n\nSo we should by default find such formulation for translation, that is more likely and in most part will survive round trip translation.\n\nWe need to make sure that all our logic related to formal translation via meta language (between programming languages, between natural languages) all have round trip translation quality test.\n\nTranslation to meta language from any other language must have zero data loss, meaning, roundtrip from meta language and back must work always.\n\nYet we also need to test roundtrips from Rust to meta language to JavaScript (roundtrip via meta language between JavaScript and Rust), from JavaScript to meta language to Rust (and round trip). The same for Russian -> meta language -> English, English -> meta language -> Russian. So Rust <-> JavaScript, Russian <-> English and so on. We should support tests for all roundtrips between each separate language and meta language, and between each 2 languages we support via meta language.\n\nWe should never support direct translation without meta language, as doing so will be computationally unsolvable.\n\nFor example if we have 100 languages, translators between them will be 100 * 100 = 10 000, and if we have 100 languages + meta language, we will need only 200 translators (to and from meta language from each 100 languages).\n\nMake sure our vision, requirements, roadmap and contributing guide all do not contradict this, and all include all relevant text about the case.\n\nWe need to collect data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), list of each and all requirements from the issue, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).\n\nPlease plan and execute everything in this single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.","closed_by":null,"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/526/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/526/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null,"pinned_comment":null}🔍 Check #4:                 3:06:33 PM

best translation is the translation that survives round trip translation.\n\nSo we should by default find such formulation for translation, that is more likely and in most part will survive round trip translation.\n\nWe need to make sure that all our logic related to formal translation via meta language (between programming languages, between natural languages) all have round trip translation quality test.\n\nTranslation to meta language from any other language must have zero data loss, meaning, roundtrip from meta language and back must work always.\n\nYet we also need to test roundtrips from Rust to meta language to JavaScript (roundtrip via meta language between JavaScript and Rust), from JavaScript to meta language to Rust (and round trip). The same for Russian -\u003e meta language -\u003e English, English -\u003e meta language -\u003e Russian. So Rust \u003c-\u003e JavaScript, Russian \u003c-\u003e English and so on. We should support tests for all roundtrips between each separate language and meta language, and between each 2 languages we support via meta language.\n\nWe should never support direct translation without meta language, as doing so will be computationally unsolvable.\n\nFor example if we have 100 languages, translators between them will be 100 * 100 = 10 000, and if we have 100 languages + meta language, we will need only 200 translators (to and from meta language from each 100 languages).\n\nMake sure our vision, requirements, roadmap and contributing guide all do not contradict this, and all include all relevant text about the case.\n\nWe need to collect data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), list of each and all requirements from the issue, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).\n\nPlease plan and execute everything in this single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.","title":"Translation quality test"}

This removes the N² anti-pattern issue #526 explicitly forbids: the translator now stays at `N` formalizers + `N` renderers, so pairs that never had a hardcoded arm (Python → JavaScript, Rust → Go) translate through one shared meaning for free.\n- Added issue #526 round-trip translation quality requirements, solution notes, online research, and raw PR/issue snapshots under `docs/case-studies/issue-526/`.\n- Expanded natural-language translation coverage so every supported language surface formalizes to the meta language and every directed en/ru/hi/zh pair round-trips through the shared meaning (already routed through `TranslationPipeline`, not direct pairs).\n- Added Rust → JavaScript → Rust code translation coverage plus a `code_translation_routes_through_the_code_meta_language_not_direct_pairs` test that proves never-hardcoded pairs translate through one shared `meaning:` link.\n- Updated `VISION.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `ROADMAP.md`, `REQUIREMENTS.md`, and `CONTRIBUTING.md` so future translation work is governed by the same round-trip, meta-language rule.\n\n## Root obsolete practice fixed\n`translate_program` previously matched on the concrete `(source, target)` language pair — a direct pair-specific translator. Issue #526 states direct translation without a meta language is computationally unsolvable (N languages → N² translators). The fix formalizes any source fragment into a `CodeMeaning` (source language irrelevant), then renders that meaning into the requested target; no `(source, target)` arm survives.\n\n## Reproduction\nBefore the fix, the reproducer failed because Rust → JavaScript returned a translation gap instead of an add-function renderer, and cross pairs like Python → JavaScript were never supported:\n\n```bash\ncargo test --test unit rust_javascript_code_translation_round_trips_through_code_meaning -- --nocapture\ncargo test --test unit code_translation_routes_through_the_code_meta_language_not_direct_pairs -- --nocapture\n```\n\n## Verification\nPost-merge verification on branch `issue-526-215ae32f521b`:\n\n```bash\ncargo test --test unit translation_round_trip -- --nocapture\ncargo test --test unit translation_via_links -- --nocapture\ncargo test --test unit issue_526_translation_quality_documents_are_traceable -- --nocapture\ncargo fmt --check\nrust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs\ncargo clippy --all-targets --all-features\ncargo test\n```\n\n## Visuals\nNot applicable; this change is tests, translation best translation is the translation that survives round trip translation.\n\nSo we should by default find such formulation for translation, that is more likely and in most part will survive round trip translation.\n\nWe need to make sure that all our logic related to formal translation via meta language (between programming languages, between natural languages) all have round trip translation quality test.\n\nTranslation to meta language from any other language must have zero data loss, meaning, roundtrip from meta language and back must work always.\n\nYet we also need to test roundtrips from Rust to meta language to JavaScript (roundtrip via meta language between JavaScript and Rust), from JavaScript to meta language to Rust (and round trip). The same for Russian -> meta language -> English, English -> meta language -> Russian. So Rust <-> JavaScript, Russian <-> English and so on. We should support tests for all roundtrips between each separate language and meta language, and between each 2 languages we support via meta language.\n\nWe should never support direct translation without meta language, as doing so will be computationally unsolvable.\n\nFor example if we have 100 languages, translators between them will be 100 * 100 = 10 000, and if we have 100 languages + meta language, we will need only 200 translators (to and from meta language from each 100 languages).\n\nMake sure our vision, requirements, roadmap and contributing guide all do not contradict this, and all include all relevant text about the case.\n\nWe need to collect data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), list of each and all requirements from the issue, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).\n\nPlease plan and execute everything in this single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.","closed_by":null,"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/526/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/526/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null,"pinned_comment":null}🔍 Check #5:                 3:08:44 PM

best translation is the translation that survives round trip translation.\n\nSo we should by default find such formulation for translation, that is more likely and in most part will survive round trip translation.\n\nWe need to make sure that all our logic related to formal translation via meta language (between programming languages, between natural languages) all have round trip translation quality test.\n\nTranslation to meta language from any other language must have zero data loss, meaning, roundtrip from meta language and back must work always.\n\nYet we also need to test roundtrips from Rust to meta language to JavaScript (roundtrip via meta language between JavaScript and Rust), from JavaScript to meta language to Rust (and round trip). The same for Russian -\u003e meta language -\u003e English, English -\u003e meta language -\u003e Russian. So Rust \u003c-\u003e JavaScript, Russian \u003c-\u003e English and so on. We should support tests for all roundtrips between each separate language and meta language, and between each 2 languages we support via meta language.\n\nWe should never support direct translation without meta language, as doing so will be computationally unsolvable.\n\nFor example if we have 100 languages, translators between them will be 100 * 100 = 10 000, and if we have 100 languages + meta language, we will need only 200 translators (to and from meta language from each 100 languages).\n\nMake sure our vision, requirements, roadmap and contributing guide all do not contradict this, and all include all relevant text about the case.\n\nWe need to collect data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), list of each and all requirements from the issue, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).\n\nPlease plan and execute everything in this single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.","title":"Translation quality test"}
